title: Building an Open, Modular, and Hackable Hardware
description: The design philosophy and engineering behind Ubo — an open, modular, and hackable hardware platform for self-hosting apps and running a customizable, privacy-first AI assistant you can actually take apart and extend.

title: The Secrets of Agile Manufacturing
description: How the Ubo open-source home pod is designed, built, and manufactured — practical techniques for small-volume production and incremental builds that cut cost, add flexibility, and make mistakes less expensive, across both electronics and mechanical parts.

title: Automated FPGA Verification and Debugging
description: A framework that automates the tedious parts of FPGA debugging and verification — mining assertions from simulation or hardware traces, generating Verilog assertion-checking engines, auto-inserting signal probes, and varying clock frequency on the fly, all while preserving the original placement. From my work as a Microsoft Research intern.
